text: Refinement of a-0005's 'never a visual property by eye': forced_visual verdict grounding is problem-structure-dependent. Content analysis of all 47 non-empty forced_visual verdicts (Studies 5+6) shows numeric grounding is near-universal on scalar-answer integral problems (1985.B5 11/11, 1993.A1 12/12 cite decimal magnitudes) but the double-inequality/ordering problem 1996.B2 is 11/12 relational ('lies above', 'sandwich', 'brackets between') and only 1/12 numeric. Overall: any-numeric 33/47, any-relational 27/47. So the verdict text DOES invoke visual/relational properties - but specifically when the claim's truth is an ordering rather than a value. This does NOT overturn modality equivalence: forced_textual caught 1996.B2 at the same rate (Study 6), so the relational 'which curve is on top' property is one a numeric sampler captures equally well. The 'never a visual property' phrasing should be narrowed to scalar-answer problems.
